doomByFateCodes.title: Doom By Fate Codes
doomByFateCodes.intro: Redeem these active codes for free Tix and exclusive skin rewards. Copy a code, open the in-game cog menu, paste it into the CODE box, and press Enter.
redeemSteps: Join the official 'what is what is' Roblox group (required for codes to work)
Launch Doom By Fate on Roblox
Click the cog icon at the bottom of the screen
Type or paste your code into the CODE box
Press Enter to claim your free reward

items.highlight: Newest working reward code — claim first for the best Tix value.
Best value code — grants both a free skin and Tix in one redemption.
A solid filler code for players saving Tix toward their first killer or survivor.
Reliable mid-value Tix pickup still active across current code trackers.

doomByFateTierList.title: Doom By Fate Tier List
doomByFateTierList.intro: A quick meta snapshot ranking survivors and killers in the current version of Doom By Fate. Use this as a fast reference before choosing your main.

tiers.summary: Jane Doe delivers top sustain and team healing, while Jester's trap kit provides layered blind and slow control that swings chases decisively.
Both bring strong frontline disruption and are effective but require more precise timing than the S-tier picks.
Bacon is the free starter survivor. Good for learning the game, but outclassed by the paid options in competitive play.
The standout killer with massive HP, anti-stun immunity, Corroded Wave zoning, and Command Grab — the most oppressive choice at all skill levels.
Jeff rewards stealth setups and Rage burst, while Captain Rampage provides consistent ranged pressure and movement burst options.
The free starting killer. Teaches core chase mechanics but has a lower ceiling than the paid killers.

doomByFateSurvivors.title: Doom By Fate Survivors
doomByFateSurvivors.intro: Compare every survivor in Doom By Fate — cost, HP, abilities, and best use case — before you spend your Tix.

items.name: Bacon
items.overview: Bacon is the free starter survivor with Flashlight (slow + blind), Pizza (heals 20 HP), and Tracker (reveals killer distance and aura pulses).
items.bestFor: New players who want map awareness and utility without spending Tix first.

items.name: Brawler
items.overview: Brawler is a frontline sentinel built around Pocket Sand and Liver Blow, dealing 50 damage with his heavy hit and keeping killers off teammates.
items.bestFor: Players who want to play aggressive bodyguard, land long stuns, and peel for their team.

items.name: Jane Doe
items.overview: Jane Doe is a support-survivalist who heals allies for 30 HP, self-heals for 35 HP, and applies a killer slow to extend or reset chases.
items.bestFor: The best first paid survivor — safest all-around value with the strongest round-long sustain.

items.name: Jester
items.overview: Jester is an ambush trickster with Hat Trap (cuts sprint), Banana Trap (ragdolls killers), and Pie (adds blind and slow) for setup-heavy play.
items.bestFor: Trap-minded players who like staging corners, stairways, and rescue paths before the killer commits.

items.name: Soldier
items.overview: Soldier is a high-HP sentinel with Carry (rescue), Block (parry), and Shotgun (short-range stun), letting him contest killer pressure directly.
items.bestFor: Players who want to babysit weaker teammates, parry pressure, and survive longer in extended chases.

items.name: Martyr
items.overview: Martyr is the newest survivor in the current [1x1x1x1 & Martyr] live build, listed alongside the new headline killer in the active game session.
items.bestFor: Players chasing the newest survivor slot in the current Doom By Fate update.

doomByFateKillers.title: Doom By Fate Killers
doomByFateKillers.intro: Every killer in Doom By Fate compared by cost, HP, pressure style, and difficulty — so you know exactly what you are buying before you spend your Tix.

items.name: C000lkid
items.overview: C000lkid is a rushdown-combo pick with wall-piercing blades, a stunning slash, a heavy punch, and a speed burst with ESP detection.
items.bestFor: Beginners who want a free killer that teaches combo routes and direct chase mechanics without spending anything.

items.name: Jeff The Killer
items.overview: Jeff is a hybrid ambusher who uses Murderous Intent for stealth, a grab for burst damage, and a Rage mode that supercharges his close-range damage output.
items.bestFor: Players who enjoy stalking first, then cashing in one explosive chase or a brutal cleanup sequence.

items.name: Captain Rampage
items.overview: Captain Rampage is built around Shark Prey Rounds, reload timing, and high-mobility attacks like Shatter Slash and Tidal Cleaver for map pressure.
items.bestFor: Players who want consistent ranged threat, burst repositioning, and stronger map control than melee killers.

items.name: John Doe?
items.overview: John Doe? is the top-tier tank killer with anti-stun immunity, Corroded Wave zoning, Command Grab, and Corrupted Force for relentless chase control.
items.bestFor: Experienced players who want the most oppressive all-around killer — the top S-tier choice in the current meta.

items.name: The Priest
items.overview: The Priest is a target-combo killer with AoE pressure and a chain built around Shriek, Pray, Crucifixion, and Snap for isolation plays.
items.bestFor: Players who like singling out one survivor, chaining crowd-control tools, and forcing awkward rescue angles for the rest of the team.

items.name: 1x1x1x1
items.overview: 1x1x1x1 (also called The Adversary or 81619) is the myth-themed headline killer of the current [1x1x1x1 & Martyr] live build.
items.bestFor: Players chasing the newest myth-styled killer in the current Doom By Fate update.

doomByFateBeginnerGuide.title: Doom By Fate Beginner Guide
doomByFateBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start with the round flow, then learn one survivor role before branching into harder kits. This version is built for players who want usable match advice instead of lore or filler.
doomByFateBeginnerGuide.intro: Doom By Fate is an 8v1 Roblox asymmetrical horror game where one Killer pressures the lobby while Survivors clear objectives, protect each other, and try to make it out before the match snowballs. The fastest way to improve is to understand your role, use map geometry well, and spend your first Tix on a playstyle you will actually repeat.

steps.title: Learn the round win condition first
steps.description: Every match is built around one Killer versus eight Survivors. Survivors win by completing objectives and escaping pressure; the Killer wins by hunting the team down before the round gets away.
highlights: Survivors should value objective time over random duels.
Killers want early pressure before routes and support tools stabilize the team.

steps.title: Use Bacon to learn information play
steps.description: Bacon is free and works as a recon-support starter. His kit teaches spacing, timing, and team awareness without forcing you into pure frontline play.
highlights: Flashlight can stagger and slow the Killer if aimed well.
Pizza heals 20 HP and helps you survive small mistakes.
Tracker helps identify the Killer's exact location and distance.

steps.title: Pick a role path before you buy anything
steps.description: The current survivor roster splits cleanly into role families. Choose one path and stay on it until you understand when to peel, heal, or disengage.
highlights: Sentinel: stuns, disruption, and frontline peel.
Support: heals, buffs, debuffs, and chase utility.

steps.title: Make your first paid survivor a purpose pick
steps.description: Jane Doe is the easiest premium pick to justify because she can heal herself and teammates while still peeling with Syringe. If you want to protect others and force space in chases, Brawler or Soldier are the cleaner frontline options.
highlights: Jane Doe: 450 Tix, self-heal plus team heal.
Brawler: 500 Tix, long stun window and strong punish.
Soldier: 750 Tix, block, carry, and shotgun control.

steps.title: Treat loops and cover as your real defense
steps.description: Good survivors do not try to win in the open. Use walls, doors, stairs, houses, decks, and choke points to stretch chase time and force bad Killer angles.
highlights: Neighborhood has many connected loop spots and strong hiding routes behind the houses.
Rampaged Ship has strong upper-deck loops and stair interactions.
Killers are much more dangerous when they catch you in open lanes.

steps.title: Turn every match into Tix progress
steps.description: Your early economy matters because survivor and killer purchases open the game up quickly. Play rounds, finish objectives, and redeem active codes when available so your first few buys come faster.
highlights: Use early Tix on a role you already enjoy.
Do not spread purchases across characters you will not main.
Codes and round success are the cleanest early progression sources.

doomByFateMaps.title: Doom By Fate Maps
doomByFateMaps.subtitle: This module turns the current map pool into quick-read match notes. Learn where to kite, hide, rotate, or cut people off before the round gets messy.
doomByFateMaps.intro: Doom By Fate maps are not just background art. Each one changes how safe your rotations feel, how much value support tools get, and whether the Killer can force a clean chase or has to keep guessing.

items.layout_notes: Old Roblox-inspired map with large outer routes, bridges, and high-ground areas around the mountain.
items.survivor_angle: Strong when you stay disciplined and use the long outer pathing to burn time.
items.killer_angle: Rush early so Survivors do not take the mountain and bridge routes for free.

items.layout_notes: Two houses, a damaged arcade, tight halls, and many connected loop spots across the block.
items.survivor_angle: One of the best maps for extended kiting, trap setups, and last-second reroutes.
items.killer_angle: Use the back side of the houses for surprise entries instead of always pathing straight through the front.

items.layout_notes: Massive damaged ship with four decks, stairs between levels, and most early action centered on the upper decks.
items.survivor_angle: Upper-deck loops and stairs are strong for resets, especially with stun or trap support.
items.killer_angle: Mobility matters here because Survivors can chain upper-deck pressure into long relos.

items.layout_notes: Large library space where tall bookshelves function like walls and sight blockers.
items.survivor_angle: Best played with disciplined pathing and line-of-sight breaks instead of panic sprinting.
items.killer_angle: Track shelf routes carefully and force commit points instead of overchasing blind corners.

items.layout_notes: Core rotation map in the current pool built around standard generator pressure and routing practice.
items.survivor_angle: Good map for learning clean objective timing and safe rotations between tasks.
items.killer_angle: Pressure generator timing early and do not let the lobby settle into free objective rhythm.

doomByFateSurvivorsTierList.title: Doom By Fate Survivors Tier List
doomByFateSurvivorsTierList.subtitle: Built around current role value, self-sufficiency, and how easy each survivor is to turn into actual wins. Aimed at players deciding where their next Tix should go.
doomByFateSurvivorsTierList.intro: The best survivor picks either keep themselves alive, rescue team momentum, or force the Killer to respect space they would normally take for free.

entries.name: Jane Doe
entries.why: Peel with Syringe, heal teammates for 30 HP, heal herself for 35 HP. The cleanest all-rounder for solo queue and team play.
entries.best_for: Players who want one survivor that stays useful in almost every lobby.

entries.name: Soldier
entries.why: Carry, Block, and Shotgun let him bodyguard, peel, and turn bad chases into recoverable situations.
entries.best_for: Players who enjoy frontline rescue, team defense, and hard peel.

entries.name: Jester
entries.why: Hat Trap kills sprint paths, Banana Trap punishes loops, and Pie adds blind plus slow. Rewards players who think ahead.

entries.name: Brawler
entries.why: Pocket Sand and Liver Blow give him one of the cleanest direct disruption kits. Excellent for aggressive peel on demand.
entries.best_for: Players who like decisive close-range saves.

entries.name: Bacon
entries.why: Teaches real fundamentals: killer tracking, spacing, and resource timing. Flashlight, Pizza, and Tracker keep him relevant while you learn.
entries.best_for: New players who want a no-cost practice character with real team value.

entries.name: Martyr
entries.why: Martyr has entered the roster as the newest survivor in the current live build. Track her page as the module ecosystem expands.
entries.best_for: Players who want the newest survivor in their rotation.

doomByFateKillersTierList.title: Doom By Fate Killers Tier List
doomByFateKillersTierList.subtitle: Focuses on chase pressure, punish windows, and how consistently each killer converts contact into downs. Made for players choosing a main.
doomByFateKillersTierList.intro: A strong Doom By Fate killer either creates unavoidable pressure or turns one mistake into a real punish sequence. The best options force bad movement, crush weak spacing, or make Survivors abandon routes they would normally trust.

entries.name: John Doe?
entries.why: Combines oppressive body presence with Corroded Wave, Command Grab, and Corrupted Force. The cleanest premium killer for top-end pressure.
entries.best_for: Players who want one expensive killer that can dominate most lobbies.

entries.name: C000lkid
entries.why: Fast, punish-heavy kit with ranged pressure, chase speed, and combo routing. For a free killer, the ceiling is absurdly high.
entries.best_for: Players who want a strong main without spending Tix first.

entries.name: Jeff The Killer
entries.why: Gets value from invisibility, front-or-back grabs, and a direct burst route with Bloodthirst. Easy to turn into fast picks.
entries.best_for: Players who like stealth entries and simple finishing power.

entries.name: The Priest
entries.why: Specializes in locking onto one target and chaining pressure through combo routing and area coverage.
entries.best_for: Players who like technical combo killers with strong target focus.

entries.name: Captain Rampage
entries.why: Excels at movement-led pressure with Shatter Slash, Tiger Mark, and Tidal Cleaver. Lethal when you keep pace high and force Survivors off safe loops.
entries.best_for: Players who enjoy mobility, tempo, and route cutting.

entries.name: 1x1x1x1
entries.why: 1x1x1x1 has entered the killer lineup with 1500 health as one of the most important current roster additions to track.
entries.best_for: Players following the newest killer coverage and update-driven pages.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Doom By Fate on Roblox

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Doom By Fate?
questions.answer: To redeem a code in Doom By Fate, join the official 'what is what is' Roblox group first. Then launch the game, click the cog icon at the bottom of the screen, type your code into the CODE box, and press Enter to claim your reward.

questions.question: What is Doom By Fate on Roblox?
questions.answer: Doom By Fate is an asymmetric horror game on Roblox inspired by games like Dead by Daylight. One player controls a killer trying to eliminate survivors, while up to five other players work together to complete objectives and escape. The game features multiple killers, survivors, and maps.

questions.question: Who are the best killers in Doom By Fate?
questions.answer: John Doe? is currently the top-tier killer in Doom By Fate thanks to his massive HP pool, anti-stun immunity, and Corroded Wave zoning. Jeff The Killer and Captain Rampage are strong A-tier picks for their burst and ranged pressure. C000lkid is the free starting killer and a solid option for beginners.

questions.question: Who are the best survivors in Doom By Fate?
questions.answer: Jane Doe and Jester are the S-tier survivors in Doom By Fate. Jane Doe excels at team healing and sustain, while Jester's traps provide strong chase control. Brawler and Soldier are reliable A-tier picks for aggressive play, and Bacon is the free starter survivor with useful utility skills.

questions.question: Is Doom By Fate free to play?
questions.answer: Yes, Doom By Fate is free to play on Roblox. The base game is accessible at no cost, with some killers and survivors available for purchase using in-game Tix currency. You can earn Tix through gameplay and by redeeming active codes.

questions.question: How many players can play Doom By Fate at once?
questions.answer: Doom By Fate supports up to 6 players per match — one killer and up to five survivors. The asymmetric setup means every match is a tense 1-vs-many experience.
